title: MCP vs API: The protocol every developer needs to know
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a protocol designed for LLMs and AI agents to autonomously discover and use tools without manual hardcoding. Unlike traditional APIs built for program-to-program communication, MCP enables models to chain tools together, interpret unstructured data, and reason about tool selection and execution through context.
writeup: This video contrasts the Model Context Protocol (MCP) with traditional APIs, explaining why a new protocol emerged for LLMs and AI agents. The core distinction: APIs are built for program-to-program communication, while MCP lets models autonomously discover and use tools without manual hardcoding. The concrete takeaways cover how MCP enables agents to chain tools together, interpret unstructured data, and reason about tool selection and execution through context.
